req.title: Message to provider (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) a valid message with a clean attachment reaches the provider and emits an AuditEvent (positive / EP), (2) a message carrying a disallowed PHI exfiltration pattern is blocked and the block itself is audited (negative / NEG + DT), and (3) a message whose text body sits exactly at the system maximum character boundary exercises the edge of the input domain (boundary / BVA). A fourth test covers the full state transition from unauthenticated → authenticated → message-sent → audit-confirmed (STC). Together the four cases give positive, negative, and boundary coverage as required by the task, while mapping every case back to R-HLT-100.

tests.name: test_send_clean_message_stores_and_audits
preconditions: Provider account exists in fhir-lite test data
No prior messages exist for the test patient in this session
actions: POST /messages with a valid text body and a clean PDF attachment
GET /messages/{id} to confirm message is stored
expected: HTTP 201 returned with message resource body
Message retrievable via GET with status=delivered or queued
At least one AuditEvent exists with action=create and outcome=success
Provider notification endpoint receives a call (stubbed via <notify_stub>)
trace: R-HLT-100 – AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_send_clean_message_stores_and_audits(auth_client, clean_pdf_attachment, notify_stub):
    payload = {
        "recipient": "provider-001",
        "body": "Follow-up question about my prescription.",
        "attachment": clean_pdf_attachment
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"

    msg_id = resp.json()["id"]

    fetch = auth_client.get(f"/messages/{msg_id}")
    assert fetch.status_code == 200
    assert fetch.json()["status"] in ("delivered", "queued")

    audit_resp = auth_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"entity": msg_id})
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert any(
        e["resource"]["action"] == "C" and e["resource"]["outcome"] == "0"
        for e in events
    ), "No successful create AuditEvent found"

    assert notify_stub.called, "Provider notification was not triggered"

tests.name: test_blocked_phi_attachment_returns_403_and_audits_block
preconditions: Attachment scanner is enabled in fhir-lite configuration
Attachment fixture <phi_attachment> contains a disallowed SSN pattern
actions: POST /messages with a text body and an attachment embedding a raw SSN (disallowed PHI pattern)
Confirm message is NOT stored via GET /messages
GET /AuditEvent?entity={correlation_id} to verify block event is audited
expected: HTTP 403 returned with error code PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED
AuditEvent exists with action=create and outcome=8 (serious failure) or equivalent rejection code
Provider notification endpoint is NOT called
trace: R-HLT-100 – blocked when disallowed attachment detected
R-HLT-100 – AuditEvent emitted for every operation (including rejections)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_blocked_phi_attachment_returns_403_and_audits_block(auth_client, phi_attachment, notify_stub):
    payload = {
        "recipient": "provider-001",
        "body": "Please see attached.",
        "attachment": phi_attachment
    }

    resp = auth_client.post("/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    error_body = resp.json()
    assert error_body.get("code") == "PHI_EXFILTRATION_DETECTED"

    correlation_id = error_body.get("correlationId")
    assert correlation_id, "Response must include a correlationId for audit tracing"

    list_resp = auth_client.get("/messages", params={"recipient": "provider-001"})
    ids = [e["resource"]["id"] for e in list_resp.json().get("entry", [])]
    assert correlation_id not in ids, "Blocked message must not be persisted"

    audit_resp = auth_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"entity": correlation_id})
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert any(
        e["resource"]["outcome"] in ("4", "8")
        for e in events
    ), "No rejection AuditEvent found for blocked message"

    assert not notify_stub.called, "Provider must NOT be notified for blocked messages"

tests.name: test_message_body_at_max_character_boundary
preconditions: System maximum message body length is known via <config_fixture> as MAX_BODY_LEN
No attachment included in this test to isolate body length variable
actions: POST /messages with body of exactly MAX_BODY_LEN characters – expect success
POST /messages with body of MAX_BODY_LEN + 1 characters – expect rejection
expected: Request at exactly MAX_BODY_LEN returns HTTP 201
AuditEvent with outcome=0 emitted for the accepted message
Request at MAX_BODY_LEN + 1 returns HTTP 422 with code BODY_TOO_LONG
AuditEvent with outcome=4 or 8 emitted for the rejected oversized message
trace: R-HLT-100 – error pathway for invalid input
R-HLT-100 – AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_message_body_at_max_character_boundary(auth_client, app_config):
    MAX_BODY_LEN = app_config["messages"]["maxBodyLength"]

    exact_body = "A" * MAX_BODY_LEN
    resp_ok = auth_client.post("/messages", json={"recipient": "provider-001", "body": exact_body})
    assert resp_ok.status_code == 201, f"Exact boundary should pass; got {resp_ok.status_code}"
    msg_id = resp_ok.json()["id"]

    audit_ok = auth_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"entity": msg_id})
    events_ok = audit_ok.json().get("entry", [])
    assert any(e["resource"]["outcome"] == "0" for e in events_ok), \
        "AuditEvent with success outcome expected for boundary-valid message"

    over_body = "A" * (MAX_BODY_LEN + 1)
    resp_fail = auth_client.post("/messages", json={"recipient": "provider-001", "body": over_body})
    assert resp_fail.status_code == 422, f"Over-boundary should fail; got {resp_fail.status_code}"
    assert resp_fail.json().get("code") == "BODY_TOO_LONG"

    corr_id = resp_fail.json().get("correlationId")
    audit_fail = auth_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"entity": corr_id})
    events_fail = audit_fail.json().get("entry", [])
    assert any(e["resource"]["outcome"] in ("4", "8") for e in events_fail), \
        "AuditEvent with failure outcome expected for over-boundary message"

tests.name: test_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_sent_to_audited
preconditions: No active session exists for the test patient
Provider account exists in fhir-lite test data
actions: Attempt POST /messages without auth token – assert 401 (state: unauthenticated)
Authenticate via POST /auth/token to obtain bearer token (state: authenticated)
POST /messages with valid body and clean attachment (state: message-sent)
Poll GET /AuditEvent until create event appears (state: audited)
expected: Authentication returns 200 with a bearer token
AuditEvent with action=C and outcome=0 is retrievable within timeout
trace: R-HLT-100 – AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

def test_state_transition_unauthenticated_to_sent_to_audited(anon_client, credentials, clean_pdf_attachment):
    unauth_resp = anon_client.post("/messages", json={"recipient": "provider-001", "body": "hello"})
    assert unauth_resp.status_code == 401, "Unauthenticated request must be rejected"

    token_resp = anon_client.post("/auth/token", json=credentials)
    assert token_resp.status_code == 200
    token = token_resp.json()["access_token"]
    assert token, "Token must be present after successful login"

    auth_client = <make_auth_client>(anon_client, token)
    send_resp = auth_client.post("/messages", json={
        "recipient": "provider-001",
        "body": "Post-authentication test message.",
        "attachment": clean_pdf_attachment
    })
    assert send_resp.status_code == 201
    msg_id = send_resp.json()["id"]

    deadline = time.time() + 10
    found = False
    while time.time() < deadline:
        audit_resp = auth_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"entity": msg_id})
        events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
        if any(e["resource"]["action"] == "C" and e["resource"]["outcome"] == "0" for e in events):
            found = True
            break
        time.sleep(0.5)
    assert found, "AuditEvent for sent message not found within timeout"
